meta_description: Learn how to idea validation and overcome can't debug code. A Intermediate-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Idea Validation: How Students Fix Debugging Roadblocks
content.seo_description: Stop spinning your wheels. Learn how rigorous idea validation and AI-assisted debugging with Antigravity and Claude Code can accelerate your dev journey.
content.answer_block: When you cannot debug, you are likely building a solution without a validated problem. By pivoting to rigorous idea validation using Antigravity and Claude Code, you isolate logic errors from architectural flaws. This approach transforms debugging from a frustrating chore into a systematic process of refining your product-market fit.
content.intro:
The screen flickers, the terminal spits out a wall of red stack traces, and the silence of your workspace feels deafening. You’ve been staring at the same function for three hours, convinced that just one more tweak will fix the logic, yet the app remains broken. You aren't just failing to code; you are hitting the wall of unvalidated complexity.

True engineering isn't about brute-forcing syntax—it’s about building things that actually matter. When you validate your ideas before writing the heavy logic, you stop chasing phantom features. Below, we’ll use Antigravity and Claude Code to dismantle your technical debt and build a foundation that actually works.

steps.title: Define the Core Hypothesis
steps.description: Before touching the code, articulate exactly what the feature should solve. If you can't explain it, you can't debug it.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Senior Product Engineer. Review my core feature hypothesis: [Insert Idea]. Identify the top 3 potential technical failure points that would make this feature impossible to implement.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Analyze my current project README and identify if the feature scope is too broad for an MVP.'

steps.title: Isolate the Failure Domain
steps.description: Use AI to determine if your bug is a syntax error or an architectural mismatch based on your validated scope.
steps.antigravity_prompt: I am stuck on this logic: [Insert Code]. Compare this against my validated scope. Is this code necessary for the MVP, or is it scope creep causing the error?

steps.title: Refactor for Testability
steps.description: Convert complex, monolithic functions into modular, testable units.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the provided code snippet into pure, testable functions. Ensure each function has a single responsibility.

steps.title: Automate Edge Case Simulation
steps.description: Use AI to generate inputs that trigger the bugs you can't find manually.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a suite of 5 edge-case inputs for this function that would likely cause it to crash.

steps.title: Final Architectural Audit
steps.description: Verify the fix aligns with the original validated idea to ensure no regression.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the recent refactor. Does it still align with the product hypothesis defined in Step 1? Identify any remaining technical debt.
steps.claude_code_command: claude commit --message 'Refactored core logic for testability and MVP alignment'

pro_tips: Treat your error logs as data, not failures. If the same error appears twice, it is an architectural signal, not a typo.
Use 'Rubber Ducking' with Claude Code; explain the code line-by-line in the terminal to force yourself to spot the logic gap.
If you find yourself writing more than 50 lines of code without testing, your scope is too large—break it down further.
Always maintain a 'Validation Log' alongside your Git commits to track why you made specific architectural decisions.
Prioritize 'Delete over Fix'. If a feature is hard to debug, it's often a sign that it shouldn't exist in your MVP.
content.next_steps: Once your code is stable, deploy your MVP to a staging environment and gather feedback from at least three real users to validate your hypothesis.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to turn your technical roadblocks into a portfolio-grade product. Stop struggling with syntax and start shipping value that users crave. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
